title: Action
description: The brain→device message: one actuation the cloud frontier loop wants performed on the target device, sent DOWN over the Tailscale 8765 channel. A discriminated union keyed on the ACTION-VARIANT discriminator `type` (element_click / element_set_text / coordinate_tap / coordinate_swipe / global_action / intent / open_app / scroll / press_key); each variant maps to a REAL entry point in the existing Android actuation layer (Actuators / IntentActuator, dispatched by AndroidPhoneController.dispatch). The MESSAGE-KIND discriminator is the SEPARATE key `msg` (const 'action' here) so it never collides with the variant `type` (I2). Coordinates on the wire are already in DEVICE SCREEN PIXELS — any provider-space denormalization (e.g. Gemini 0-999 → px) happens server-side in the M7 coordinate adapter before this message is emitted. TRANSPORT ENVELOPE (M2): on the wire a POST /action body is this action.json payload PLUS the sibling transport keys `task_id` + `operator` (the correlation id + the operator scope). A strict validator should validate the action VARIANT against this schema after stripping those two framing keys (see docs/schema/README.md → Transport envelope).

msg.$comment: MESSAGE-KIND discriminator (I2). Const 'action' for every action frame. DISTINCT from each variant's own `type` (the ACTION-VARIANT discriminator, element_click / …) so the two discriminators never collide on the wire.
msg.description: Message kind — always 'action' for a brain→device action frame (vs 'observation' / 'action_result').

element_ref.$comment: Shared element addressing: resource_id (preferred, stable) OR node_id (positional fallback). parseNodeRef() prefers resource_id when both are given.

element_click.title: element_click
element_click.$comment: ROUTES TO: AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("tap") → parseNodeRef(args) → Actuators.tap(NodeRef). Performs a semantic ACTION_CLICK on the resolved node (or nearest clickable ancestor); coordinate-gesture center-tap only if nothing in the ancestor chain is clickable. High-consequence taps are autonomy-gated inside Actuators.

resource_id.description: Stable viewIdResourceName from the observation (PREFERRED). → NodeRef.ById → UiTreeReader.findNodeByResourceId.

node_id.description: Positional DFS index fallback (for nodes with no resource_id). → NodeRef.ByIndex → UiTreeReader.findActionableNode.

element_set_text.title: element_set_text
element_set_text.$comment: ROUTES TO: AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("type") → parseNodeRef(args) + text → Actuators.type(NodeRef, text) → ACTION_SET_TEXT. CREDENTIAL-HANDOFF GATED: if the resolved node is a password field, `text` is DISCARDED and the USER is asked to type the secret directly — the model's text is never entered, logged, or forwarded.

text.description: Text to set on the editable node. On a password field this is discarded (credential handoff) — never send a real secret here; the model must not know it.

coordinate_tap.title: coordinate_tap
coordinate_tap.$comment: ROUTES TO: dispatchGesture single-tap at (x,y). NOTE (M0/M1 gap): the existing Actuators.dispatchTap(svc,x,y) is PRIVATE and only reachable as the internal fallback inside Actuators.tap(NodeRef); there is NO public coordinate-tap entry point yet, and AndroidPhoneController has no "coordinate_tap" branch. M1 (Task 1.3) must expose a public Actuators.tap(x,y) + add the dispatch branch. This schema defines the CONTRACT the wiring builds against.

coordinate_swipe.title: coordinate_swipe
coordinate_swipe.$comment: ROUTES TO: Actuators.swipe(startX,startY,endX,endY) (public, overlay/Actuators.kt:278) → dispatchSwipe → dispatchGesture. NOTE (M0/M1 gap): AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("swipe") today takes only a `direction` string, not coordinates — M1 must add a coordinate branch that calls the existing public swipe(x,y,x2,y2). duration_ms maps to the stroke duration (default SWIPE_DURATION_MS=250).

x.description: Start X in device screen pixels.

y.description: Start Y in device screen pixels.

x2.description: End X in device screen pixels.

y2.description: End Y in device screen pixels.

duration_ms.description: Swipe stroke duration in ms (StrokeDescription duration). Defaults to Actuators.SWIPE_DURATION_MS = 250.

global_action.title: global_action
global_action.$comment: ROUTES TO: performGlobalAction. back → AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("back") → Actuators.back() → GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK; home → dispatch("home") → Actuators.home() → GLOBAL_ACTION_HOME. NOTE (M0/M1 gap): `recents` is in this contract enum but is NOT wired today — globalActionFor() maps ONLY "back"/"home" (overlay/Actuators.kt:561) and AndroidPhoneController has no "recents" branch. M1 must add recents → GLOBAL_ACTION_RECENTS.

action.description: System navigation action performed via AccessibilityService.performGlobalAction.

intent.title: intent
intent.$comment: ROUTES TO: AndroidPhoneController.dispatch(name) → (name in ResidentTools.INTENT_ACTIONS) → IntentActuator.perform(name, params). Each fires a deterministic single-shot stock-Android OS intent (or the flashlight torch / the guarded send_intent escape-hatch). send_email / send_sms / send_intent are autonomy-gated (HIGH-CONSEQUENCE — confirm-gated in Permission mode) inside IntentActuator. NOTE (I1, wire-safety): the on-device parser (RemoteActionChannel.parseAction) REJECTS any `name` not in ResidentTools.INTENT_ACTIONS with error 'unknown_action', so a gesture/global/coordinate dispatch name (read_screen, coordinate_tap, tap, back, …) can NOT be smuggled through the intent branch (type-confusion) to bypass the coordinate capability-gate or leak screen text. Per-name params: send_email{to, subject?, body?}; send_sms{number, body?}; show_map{query}; open_url{uri (http/https OR app deep-link scheme — file/content/intent/javascript/data are rejected), url alias}; dial{number}; set_alarm{hour, minutes, label?}; set_timer{seconds, label?}; share_text{text}; create_contact{first_name?, last_name?, phone_number?, email?}; create_calendar_event{datetime, title?}; open_settings_panel{which?} (LEGACY — kept for back-compat; superseded by open_settings); open_wifi_settings{}; take_photo{}; flashlight_on{}; flashlight_off{}; capture_video{}; show_alarms{}; view_calendar{datetime?}; pick_contact{}; view_contacts{}; pick_file{mime?}; create_document{mime?, filename?}; navigate{destination}; play_media{query}; open_settings{panel} (SUPERSEDES open_settings_panel — opens ANY Settings.ACTION_* panel by key); send_intent{action, uri?, mime?, package?, extras?} (GUARDED generic escape-hatch: dangerous-action denylist + unsafe-URI-scheme reject + high-consequence confirm-gate). NOTE: open_app is NOT an intent — it is the separate open_app PHONE_ACTUATOR variant (routes to Actuators.openApp), so it is deliberately absent from this enum.

name.description: Intent action name — MUST be one of ResidentTools.INTENT_ACTIONS (both the on-device parser and the overlay routing reject anything else — parser: 'unknown_action'; overlay: 'unknown intent action').

params.description: The intent's arguments, passed verbatim as the JsonObject args to IntentActuator.perform. Keys are per-name (see $comment). IntentActuator validates required args at runtime and returns a structured '<key> required' error if missing.

open_app.title: open_app
open_app.$comment: ROUTES TO: AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("open_app") → args["package"] → Actuators.openApp(packageName) — overlay/Actuators.kt:317. Fires the package's launch Intent (FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK); an uninstalled package returns success=false 'app not installed: <pkg>'. This is a PHONE_ACTUATOR (ResidentTools.PHONE_ACTUATORS), NOT an intent action, so it is a top-level action variant and NOT in intent.name. WIRE (I2): the remote frame key is EXACTLY `package` (additionalProperties:false) — the parser no longer accepts a `package_name` alias on the wire.

package.description: Android application package id to launch, e.g. 'com.google.android.apps.maps'. → Actuators.openApp(packageName) → packageManager.getLaunchIntentForPackage.

scroll.title: scroll
scroll.$comment: ROUTES TO: AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("scroll") → args["direction"] → Actuators.scroll(direction) — overlay/Actuators.kt:297 → swipeCoords(direction) centered-swipe via dispatchGesture. Direction-based (NOT coordinate-based) so it stays XR-portable — it needs no flat framebuffer coordinate space, only a cardinal direction. Unknown direction returns success=false 'unknown scroll direction: <dir>'.

direction.description: Cardinal scroll direction (== finger drag direction; a 'up' scroll drags from lower-center to upper-center). Matched case-insensitively by swipeCoords; anything outside this enum is rejected on-device.

press_key.title: press_key
press_key.$comment: ROUTES TO: AndroidPhoneController.dispatch("press_key") → args["key"] → Actuators.pressKey(key). `enter` submits the currently-focused editable field via AccessibilityNodeInfo.AccessibilityAction.ACTION_IME_ENTER (API 30+; graceful 'no focused field'/'not supported' otherwise) — this is how a 'type → submit' flow (a search box, a chat input) completes without a coordinate. `back`/`home`/`recents` reuse the performGlobalAction path (globalActionFor) — same effect as the global_action variant, exposed here as a 'key' so a model that thinks in keystrokes lands correctly. `tab`/`delete` are best-effort (no reliable global/IME equivalent) → `success=false, 'unsupported key: <k>'` in M2. CAPABILITY-SAFE: press_key uses NO coordinates, so it is meaningful on every form factor incl. XR (never coordinate-gated). WIRE-SAFETY: RemoteActionChannel.parseAction validates `key` against this enum and rejects anything else with 'invalid_argument'.

key.description: The key to press. `enter` → ACTION_IME_ENTER on the focused editable node (submit); `back`/`home`/`recents` → performGlobalAction; `tab`/`delete` → best-effort (unsupported in M2). Matched case-insensitively on-device; anything outside this enum is rejected as invalid_argument.
